meta.title: D7460N | Browser-Native Architecture
meta.description: Structural simplification of modern web applications through strict separation of concerns.

intro: Performance, accessibility, and security by default.

content: Modern web applications have accumulated unnecessary complexity through dependency stacking, framework churn, and duplicated front-end implementations across projects. This approach rethinks that pattern at the structural level.

Instead of coupling presentation to runtime logic, the browser itself becomes the rendering engine in its purest form. HTML defines structure. CSS governs visibility and state. JavaScript is restricted to data retrieval and delivery only.
This architectural discipline allows multiple datasets to render uniquely while sharing a single structural skeleton. The result is flexibility without duplication.
By reducing dependency chains and runtime mutation, the surface area for failure decreases dramatically. Maintenance effort drops while performance and accessibility improve by default.
The long-term impact is cumulative stability. What was once repeated across dozens or hundreds of projects becomes centralized, predictable, and governable.

outro: Structural discipline is not a limitation. It is leverage.
The browser already contains the power required. The architecture simply respects it.
